# Inherited compliance checklist

Every generated GPT must record pass/fail evidence for this single checklist. Package-specific controls may add requirements but cannot remove these.

- [ ] Audience, public/internal boundary, data classification, and allowed Actions are explicit.
- [ ] Owner and any assigned licensed lending partner resolve to active approved profiles.
- [ ] Mortgage identity, NMLS, Equal Housing, contact, website, Apply Now, and disclosure text come only from generated Identity Pack output.
- [ ] No unsupported mortgage advertising, rate, APR, payment, fee, savings, program, approval, qualification, licensing, or guarantee claim appears.
- [ ] RESPA, Fair Housing, Equal Housing, licensing, trigger-term, CAN-SPAM/TCPA, privacy, and jurisdiction requirements are reviewed where applicable.
- [ ] AI assistance is not represented as a licensed person or decision-maker; required AI disclosure is present.
- [ ] Public assistants cannot reach borrower records, pipeline data, pricing engines, internal guidelines, or internal-only tools.
- [ ] Lead capture identifies the licensed recipient and requires explicit affirmative consent before the Action call.
- [ ] Sensitive application data and documents are refused in chat and redirected to the secure approved application path.
- [ ] Refusal, escalation, Action failure, stale-source, ambiguity, and human-handoff behavior are tested.
- [ ] Every volatile knowledge source has an owner, effective date, review date, jurisdiction, and expiration behavior.
- [ ] A named compliance reviewer and launch owner approve the package; unresolved failures block release.
